text:
In the dense rainforest hidden within a remote island nation, a shy squirrel named Poppy spends her days hopping from walnut to walnut. Each evening, she glances longingingly through the leaves at the moon.

One night, under a full moon, she stumbles upon an ancient tree trunk. Nestled there were strange symbols—and then, something shining. Poppy hid excitedly behind a branch.

The moonlight danced around a small, glowing stone. Poppy reached out and took it home.

That stone soon turned out to be an enchanted gem. Every night, a fairy would land near it. They loved storytelling, and Poppy listened for hours—all about mice who transformed into knights and dragons.

One night, when no fairy could be found, Poppy sat pondering. She imagined herself a brave dragon. With wings of fluttering leaves, she roamed the forest. On her flight abroad, she found a shiny brass star.
